# Performance Reference

Use this reference when optimizing load times, runtime performance, or bundle size.
Performance is not a phase — it is an architectural concern that affects every decision.

> **Framework-specific performance:** This file covers React/Next.js patterns.
> For Vue/Nuxt performance (routeRules, shallowRef, v-memo, NuxtImg), see `references/performance-vue.md`.
> For Svelte, Angular, and Astro, see the Performance section in each framework skill.

---

## Core Web Vitals

The three metrics that Google uses to measure real-world user experience.

### LCP — Largest Contentful Paint

**What:** Time until the largest visible element (image, heading, text block) finishes rendering.
**Target:** Under 2.5 seconds.

Common causes of poor LCP:
- Unoptimized hero images
- Render-blocking CSS or JS
- Slow server response (TTFB)
- Client-side rendering of above-the-fold content
- Web fonts blocking text render

Fixes:
```tsx
// Preload critical images
<link rel="preload" as="image" href="/hero.webp" />

// In Next.js — use priority for above-the-fold images
import Image from "next/image";
<Image src="/hero.webp" alt="..." priority />

// Server-render the critical content — don't hydrate it client-side
// Use Server Components for above-the-fold content
async function HeroSection() {
  const content = await getHeroContent(); // Runs on server
  return (
    <section>
      <h1>{content.title}</h1>
      <p>{content.subtitle}</p>
    </section>
  );
}
```

### CLS — Cumulative Layout Shift

**What:** How much the page layout shifts unexpectedly during load.
**Target:** Under 0.1.

Common causes of poor CLS:
- Images without dimensions
- Ads or embeds that load late
- Dynamically injected content above existing content
- Web fonts causing text reflow

Fixes:
```tsx
// Always set dimensions on images
<Image src="/photo.jpg" alt="..." width={800} height={600} />

// Or use aspect-ratio in CSS
<div style={{ aspectRatio: "16/9" }}>
  <img src="/photo.jpg" alt="..." style={{ width: "100%", height: "100%" }} />
</div>

// Reserve space for async content
function AdSlot() {
  return <div style={{ minHeight: 250 }} aria-hidden="true" />;
}

// Use font-display: swap to prevent invisible text
@font-face {
  font-family: "Brand";
  src: url("/fonts/brand.woff2") format("woff2");
  font-display: swap;
}
```

### INP — Interaction to Next Paint

**What:** Time from user interaction (click, tap, key) to the next visual update.
**Target:** Under 200ms.

Common causes of poor INP:
- Expensive re-renders on interaction
- Synchronous state updates that block the main thread
- Large DOM trees (10,000+ nodes)
- Third-party scripts blocking the main thread

Fixes:
```tsx
// Use useTransition for non-urgent updates
const [isPending, startTransition] = useTransition();

function handleFilter(value: string) {
  setInput(value); // Urgent — update input immediately
  startTransition(() => {
    setFilteredResults(expensiveFilter(data, value)); // Non-urgent
  });
}

// Use useDeferredValue for expensive child renders
const deferredQuery = useDeferredValue(query);
<HeavyList query={deferredQuery} />

// Virtualize long lists
import { useVirtualizer } from "@tanstack/react-virtual";
```

---

## Rendering Strategy

Choose the right rendering strategy per page.

| Strategy | When | Next.js |
|---|---|---|
| **SSG** (Static Site Generation) | Content doesn't change often (marketing, docs, blog) | Default for pages without dynamic data |
| **ISR** (Incremental Static Regeneration) | Content changes periodically, but not per-request | `revalidate` option in fetch or route config |
| **SSR** (Server-Side Rendering) | Content is personalized or changes per-request | `dynamic = "force-dynamic"` or uncached fetch |
| **CSR** (Client-Side Rendering) | Interactive dashboards, user-specific real-time data | `"use client"` + data fetching library |
| **Streaming** | Page has mixed critical and non-critical data | `<Suspense>` boundaries |

```tsx
// SSG — cached at build time
async function BlogPost({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) {
  const { slug } = await params;
  const post = await getPost(slug); // Cached at build
  return <Article post={post} />;
}

// ISR — revalidate every 60 seconds
async function ProductPage({ params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }) {
  const { id } = await params;
  const product = await fetch(`/api/products/${id}`, {
    next: { revalidate: 60 },
  }).then((r) => r.json());
  return <ProductDetail product={product} />;
}

// Streaming — critical content first, non-critical streams in
async function DashboardPage() {
  return (
    <main>
      <DashboardHeader /> {/* Renders immediately */}
      <Suspense fallback={<ChartSkeleton />}>
        <SlowChart /> {/* Streams when ready */}
      </Suspense>
    </main>
  );
}
```

---

## Image Optimization

### Next.js Image Component

```tsx
import Image from "next/image";

// Above the fold — preload
<Image src="/hero.webp" alt="..." width={1200} height={600} priority />

// Below the fold — lazy load (default)
<Image src="/product.webp" alt="..." width={400} height={300} />

// Fill container
<div style={{ position: "relative", aspectRatio: "16/9" }}>
  <Image src="/banner.webp" alt="..." fill style={{ objectFit: "cover" }} />
</div>

// With blur placeholder
<Image
  src="/photo.webp"
  alt="..."
  width={800}
  height={600}
  placeholder="blur"
  blurDataURL={base64BlurHash}
/>
```

### General Rules

- Use modern formats: WebP or AVIF (40–60% smaller than JPEG/PNG).
- Serve responsive sizes via `srcset` and `sizes`.
- Lazy load all images below the fold.
- Set explicit `width` and `height` to prevent CLS.
- Use `priority` or `fetchpriority="high"` for LCP images.
- Compress images — a 5MB hero image is never acceptable.

---

## Font Loading

### Self-Hosted Fonts (Preferred)

```css
@font-face {
  font-family: "Brand";
  src: url("/fonts/brand-regular.woff2") format("woff2");
  font-weight: 400;
  font-style: normal;
  font-display: swap; /* Show fallback immediately, swap when loaded */
}

@font-face {
  font-family: "Brand";
  src: url("/fonts/brand-bold.woff2") format("woff2");
  font-weight: 700;
  font-style: normal;
  font-display: swap;
}
```

### Next.js Font Optimization

```tsx
import { Inter } from "next/font/google";

const inter = Inter({
  subsets: ["latin"],
  display: "swap",
  variable: "--font-inter",
});

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" className={inter.variable}>
      <body>{children}</body>
    </html>
  );
}
```

### `font-display` Values

| Value | Behavior | Use When |
|---|---|---|
| `swap` | Show fallback immediately, swap when loaded | Most cases — best for LCP |
| `optional` | Show fallback, only swap if loaded very quickly | Brand font not critical for readability |
| `block` | Invisible text for up to 3s, then swap | Icon fonts (but prefer SVG icons) |
| `fallback` | Brief invisible text (~100ms), then fallback, late swap | Balanced approach |

Rules:
- Use `woff2` format — best compression, widest support.
- Self-host over CDN — eliminates a third-party connection.
- Subset fonts to only the characters you need.
- Preload critical fonts: `<link rel="preload" as="font" href="/fonts/brand.woff2" crossorigin />`
- Limit to 2–3 font families maximum.

---

## Bundle Optimization

### Analyzing Bundle Size

```bash
# Next.js
ANALYZE=true next build

# Vite
npx vite-bundle-visualizer

# Generic
npx source-map-explorer dist/**/*.js
```

### Common Optimizations

```tsx
// Dynamic import for heavy components
const ChartLibrary = React.lazy(() => import("./Chart"));

// Import only what you need
import { format } from "date-fns/format";       // ~1KB
// Not: import { format } from "date-fns";       // ~70KB

// Use native APIs instead of libraries
// Instead of lodash.debounce:
function debounce<T extends (...args: unknown[]) => void>(fn: T, ms: number) {
  let timer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>;
  return (...args: Parameters<T>) => {
    clearTimeout(timer);
    timer = setTimeout(() => fn(...args), ms);
  };
}

// Instead of moment.js:
new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US", { dateStyle: "medium" }).format(date);

// Instead of lodash.cloneDeep:
structuredClone(obj);
```

### Budget Guidelines

| Resource | Budget |
|---|---|
| Total JS (compressed) | < 200KB for initial load |
| Any single dependency | Check on bundlephobia.com before adding |
| First-party JS | < 100KB compressed |
| CSS | < 50KB compressed |
| Fonts | < 100KB total |
| LCP image | < 200KB |

---

## Third-Party Scripts

Third-party scripts are the #1 cause of performance problems on most sites.

```tsx
// Load non-critical scripts with next/script
import Script from "next/script";

// Analytics — load after page is interactive
<Script src="https://analytics.example.com/script.js" strategy="afterInteractive" />

// Chat widget — load when idle
<Script src="https://chat.example.com/widget.js" strategy="lazyOnload" />

// Critical third-party — load before interactive (rare)
<Script src="https://cdn.example.com/critical.js" strategy="beforeInteractive" />
```

Rules:
- Audit all third-party scripts — each one impacts INP and LCP.
- Use `afterInteractive` or `lazyOnload` strategy for non-critical scripts.
- Self-host critical third-party code when possible.
- Set up a Content Security Policy (CSP) to control which scripts can execute.
- Monitor third-party impact with Web Vitals reporting.

---

## Runtime Performance

### Avoiding Layout Thrashing

```tsx
// Bad — reads and writes interleaved cause forced reflows
elements.forEach((el) => {
  const height = el.offsetHeight; // Read
  el.style.height = `${height + 10}px`; // Write
});

// Good — batch reads, then batch writes
const heights = elements.map((el) => el.offsetHeight); // All reads
elements.forEach((el, i) => {
  el.style.height = `${heights[i] + 10}px`; // All writes
});
```

### Virtual Scrolling for Long Lists

```tsx
import { useVirtualizer } from "@tanstack/react-virtual";

function VirtualList({ items }: { items: Item[] }) {
  const parentRef = React.useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

  const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({
    count: items.length,
    getScrollElement: () => parentRef.current,
    estimateSize: () => 64,
  });

  return (
    <div ref={parentRef} style={{ height: 400, overflow: "auto" }}>
      <div style={{ height: virtualizer.getTotalSize() }}>
        {virtualizer.getVirtualItems().map((virtualItem) => (
          <div
            key={virtualItem.key}
            style={{
              position: "absolute",
              top: virtualItem.start,
              height: virtualItem.size,
              width: "100%",
            }}
          >
            <ItemRow item={items[virtualItem.index]} />
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Virtualize when:
- Lists have 100+ items.
- Each item has significant DOM complexity.
- Users rarely need to see all items at once.

---

## Monitoring

### Web Vitals Reporting

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx — report Web Vitals
import { useReportWebVitals } from "next/web-vitals";

export function WebVitalsReporter() {
  useReportWebVitals((metric) => {
    // Send to your analytics
    analytics.track("web-vital", {
      name: metric.name,    // LCP, CLS, INP, FCP, TTFB
      value: metric.value,
      rating: metric.rating, // "good", "needs-improvement", "poor"
    });
  });

  return null;
}
```

### Performance Budget in CI

Set up performance budgets that fail the build when exceeded:
- Maximum JS bundle size per route
- Maximum total transfer size
- Core Web Vitals thresholds via Lighthouse CI

---

## Checklist

- [ ] LCP image uses `priority` or `fetchpriority="high"`
- [ ] All images have explicit dimensions (no CLS)
- [ ] Fonts use `font-display: swap` and are self-hosted or optimized
- [ ] Non-critical JS is code-split or lazy-loaded
- [ ] No unnecessary client-side rendering for static content
- [ ] Heavy computations use `useTransition` or `useDeferredValue`
- [ ] Long lists use virtualization
- [ ] Third-party scripts are loaded with appropriate strategy
- [ ] Bundle size is within budget
- [ ] Web Vitals are monitored in production
